summary: LOTTOLAB SaaS Enterprise Iteration 12 Testing Complete - 24/25 backend tests passed (96%), 16/16 frontend tests passed (100%). Tested company lifecycle (soft delete, suspension, activation, restore), subscription counter display, staff management CRUD, RBAC permissions API, and edit modal fields.

minor.issue: 500 error when updating status to SUSPENDED - TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes

issues: Shows 'Failed to load dashboard data' toast but still displays zeros - cosmetic issue, doesn't break functionality

action_items: Fix datetime comparison bug in PUT /api/saas/companies/{company_id} when setting status to SUSPENDED - line 1093 in saas_core.py has offset-naive vs offset-aware datetime comparison
seed_data_creation: Test companies created during testing: TEST_DELETE_*, TEST_SUSPEND_* (automatically cleaned up). Test staff members created and deleted during staff management tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All SaaS Enterprise features tested. Cron job configured correctly for daily subscription expiration at 00:00. Subscription counter displays on Company Admin dashboard with dynamic color coding (green >15 days, yellow 5-15 days, red <5 days). Staff management CRUD working. One minor datetime bug in company status update API.

cron_job_system.configuration: PASS - Scheduler configured with APScheduler at 00:00 daily
cron_job_system.startup_check: PASS - Initial check runs on server startup

company_soft_delete.status_set_to_deleted: PASS - Company status correctly set to DELETED
company_soft_delete.users_blocked: PASS - All company users are suspended
company_soft_delete.admin_login_blocked: PASS - Company admin cannot login after deletion

company_suspension.users_suspended: PASS - All users are suspended when company is suspended
company_suspension.login_blocked: PASS - Suspended company admin cannot login

company_activation.login_restored: PASS - Admin can login after activation

company_restore.new_subscription: PASS - Sets new subscription end date

edit_modal.status_update: FAIL - 500 error due to datetime bug
edit_modal.ui_fields: PASS - All edit modal fields visible and functional

subscription_counter.dashboard_display: PASS - Shows on Company Admin dashboard
subscription_counter.days_remaining: PASS - Displays remaining days with color coding
subscription_counter.plan_name: PASS - Displays plan name (Professional, Basic, etc)

frontend_ui.super_admin_companies: PASS - Table shows companies with action buttons
frontend_ui.create_company_modal: PASS - Form fields present and functional
frontend_ui.edit_company_modal: PASS - All fields including status dropdown visible

rca_of_issue: The datetime bug occurs in saas_core.py around line 1093 when comparing license_end (which may be a string ISO timestamp from MongoDB) with datetime.now(timezone.utc). The fromisoformat parsing creates an offset-aware datetime, but somewhere in the code path, an offset-naive datetime is being compared. The fix would be to ensure all datetime comparisons use timezone-aware datetimes or to standardize on UTC.
